sections.paragraph:
Αὔγουστος, ου, ὁ (so accented L-S-J-M, DGE; on the sp. Ἄγους-s. M-M) Augustus, title (usu. transl. Σεβαστός, q.v.) given Octavian, first Rom. emperor (31 b.c.–14 a.d.) in 27 b.c.; Lk 2:1. Ἀόστου GJs 17:1.—HDieckmann, Kaisernamen u. Kaiserbezeichnungen b. Lk: ZKT 33, 1919, 213–34; EPeterson, Kaiser A. im Urteil d. antiken Christent.: Hochland 30, ’33, 289ff; RAC I 933–1004.—M-M.

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

DGE DGE = Diccionario Griego-Español—List 6

  1. sp. = spelling

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

usu. usu. = usually

transl. transl. = translate, translation

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

ZKT ZKT = Zeitschrift für katholische Theologie—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 150
